title: Random Goodies That Have Helped So Far
body:
Cancer context: T2N1M0 TNBC, diagnosed Feb 5, confirmed TNBC Feb 13, started keynote 522 March 5, chemo port installed March 15. I am 38 with a child under 5.

Here are some random things that don’t frequently come up in lists that I have found so helpful even this early in my cancer journey:

- H&M nursing [camisole](https://www2.hm.com/en_ca/productpage.0534164001.html) for my port surgery and for chemo. Cheap, comfy, can just unclip the relevant side and layer with a loose Oxford shirt that I can button or unbutton. Wore it for my port surgery and was so easy to dress myself, comfy, and still felt kinda cute which I know is not a priority but helped with my dignity.


- a soft [hairband](https://www.reitmans.com/en/sherpa-headband/475836.html) that you can wear out/not just for face washing. It was great to have something to keep my hair out of my face while I can’t lift my arms and was very comfy to wear. Also helped hide my grease as I am not washing it as frequently. I expect my hair to begin falling out soon so I wasn’t prepared to spend much money on hair stuff and this one that I found is less than $5 and cute (Sherpa!)


- [pulparindo](https://a.co/d/iJK0AaK) watermelon tamarind candy. There are other flavours but I feel strongly this is the tangiest and least spicy. It has everything - sour, sweet, spicy, salty, gets rid of any gross chemo flavours and stimulates saliva AND has fibre AND is a natural but powerful laxative (tamarind). Don’t do what I did and eat 8 in one evening unless you want to poop your brains out.


- these [pur](https://shop.shoppersdrugmart.ca/pur-mellow-eyes-hemp-infused-eye-patches/p/BB_847137057237?variantCode=847137057237&utm_source=google&utm_medium=search&utm_campaign=2024~1~4~ld~external~shoppersdrugmart~na~sdm~sdm-beauty~national~pmax~7000545-2023-sdm-ecomm-ao&utm_content=6080009~2024-sdm-ecomm-ao-pmax-prestige-en~na~en&utm_term=&gbraid=0AAAAADgnWbuAvEF1K_WNcDrp29ZQwNMyE&gclid=Cj0KCQjwqdqvBhCPARIsANrmZhNYXQWucFEIjhM4x2gEtPHn32fn0CFWantiOcUaqQX7JBdfUwRZxCMaAtwIEALw_wcB) eye patches that don’t irritate my skin, and actually brighten under my eyes and make them look less pouchy, dark, and tired. I have super dark circles at the best of times and these really do a lot to help me look and feel a bit more refreshed when I’m in my chemo weeks.


- this is my cancer [notebook](https://rhodiapads.com/collections_premium_goalbook.php) and I love it. Dotted pages, multiple calendars for appointments, a pocket for records, table of contents, two book marks, and lovely to write on. I tape every provider’s business card in and track all my appointments and questions and life notes here.


- these [folders](https://a.co/d/ejzVVIY) for organizing all the admin that comes with having cancer. One for my medical care (prescriptions, hospital handout materials, doctor notes, copies of my imaging records, etc.) one for medical expenses (new, submitted to my insurance, submitted to my husband’s insurance, action required, and complete). One for legal/financial things (will, insurance, taxes, etc.) as well as cards form friends and family and thank you cards for me to use. And one for my son (he is with me away from home while I undergo treatment so he can be with my family).


- most random of all…[walkie talkies](https://a.co/d/5g4pmek) for me and my son. Allows me to be in my bedroom to rest or be sick but help him feel like he can access me or just ‘call’ me whenever he needs (my house layout is weird, and these also help him feel less alone/that I am nearby. Please know he is with my parents and siblings and never just hanging out alone. But there is no substitute for mum. So I can be sick, or rest, or take a breather, and he knows he can talk to me when he needs to if they happen to coincide). They have no background noise and the sound quality is amazing. My son loves his and loves to just use it to randomly say “I love you mummy, have a good rest!” 🥲 and helps this feel like…less serious, if that makes sense? Or that he and I have our own special thing that he can use as he likes 💓


- CBD oil (a low dose one because I am sensitive) to help with sleep, anxiety, nausea, appetite, and pain. Better than anything I have been prescribed. Cannabis is legal where I am so it is very accessible and affordable for medical needs.


- [Restoralax](https://shop.shoppersdrugmart.ca/restoralax-restoralax-powder-laxative-effective-relief-no-taste-no-grit-no-gas-no-bloating-no-cramps-convenient-10-single-dose-sachets/p/RP15?variantCode=056219823916&utm_source=google&utm_medium=search&utm_campaign=2022~12~22~ld~external~shoppersdrugmart~na~sdm~sdm-frontshop~national~shopping~2023-sdm-ecomm-ao&utm_content=6014349~2023-sdm-ecomm-ao-pmax-frontshop-en~na~en&utm_term=&gbraid=0AAAAADgnWbvT1QcTTPCHcx4sEe9C76iDU&gclid=Cj0KCQjwqdqvBhCPARIsANrmZhMjCpLN6Hsf4-YsIFFFq3LWvkj-V9PH470f-h6H9m0n1FWZLQPKNZ4aAk5iEALw_wcB). Just take it everyday, safe to use long term. I had to take it throughout my pregnancy and now it is a trusted friend.


- Short stories. In particular, [Alice Munro](https://a.co/d/8mh8dSa). Reading about the lives of women from this Nobel prize winner have been so insightful, soothing, and the short story format is perfect for waiting for appointments, trying to fall asleep, etc. Breast cancer, for me, is very much connected to my experience as a woman and Alice Munro’s stories and style are even more poignant and resonant and calming than previously (I have loved her writing for a long time.)


These all sound so frivolous but they are little things that have helped me feel dignified and organized even when I am actually anything but and am on a tight budget. Wanted to share to the wonderful community here. ❤️
